questions.question: Who established ‘Rehnumai Mazdayasan Sabha’ in 1851? > 1851 में 'रहनुमाई मजदायसन सभा' की स्थापना किसने की थी?
questions.explanation: The Rehnumai Mazdayasan Sabha, established in 1851, was founded by prominent Parsis such as Dadabhai Naoroji, Naoroji Furdonji, S.S. Bengalee, and others. This association aimed to reform and modernize Parsi society, focusing on education, women's rights, and religious reform. > 1851 में स्थापित रहनुमाई मजदायसन सभा की स्थापना दादाभाई नौरोजी, नौरोजी फुरदोंजी, एसएस बंगाली और अन्य प्रमुख पारसियों ने की थी। इस संघ का उद्देश्य शिक्षा, महिलाओं के अधिकारों और धार्मिक सुधार पर ध्यान केंद्रित करते हुए पारसी समाज में सुधार और आधुनिकीकरण करना था।

questions.question: . What is India’s rank in World Press Freedom Index 2024? > विश्व प्रेस स्वतंत्रता सूचकांक 2024 में भारत की रैंक क्या है?
questions.explanation: The World Press Freedom Index 2024, published by Reporters without Borders [RSF], highlights the state of press freedom globally. Norway, Denmark, and Sweden retained the top three positions, showcasing strong press freedom. India improved slightly, moving from 161st in 2023 to 159th among 180 countries in 2024, but its overall score declined due to challenges like political and economic pressures. Eritrea remained at the bottom of the list. >रिपोर्टर्स विदाउट बॉर्डर्स [RSF] द्वारा प्रकाशित विश्व प्रेस स्वतंत्रता सूचकांक 2024, विश्व स्तर पर प्रेस स्वतंत्रता की स्थिति पर प्रकाश डालता है। नॉर्वे, डेनमार्क और स्वीडन ने मजबूत प्रेस स्वतंत्रता का प्रदर्शन करते हुए शीर्ष तीन स्थानों को बरकरार रखा। भारत में थोड़ा सुधार हुआ, जो वर्ष 2023 में 161वें से वर्ष 2024 में 180 देशों में 159वें स्थान पर पहुँच गया, लेकिन राजनीतिक और आर्थिक दबावों जैसी चुनौतियों के कारण इसके समग्र स्कोर में गिरावट आई। इरिट्रिया सूची में सबसे नीचे रहा।

questions.question: India shares his border with 09 neighbouring countries including _____ sea borders and ______ land borders respectively? > भारत क्रमशः _____ समुद्री सीमाओं और ______ भूमि सीमाओं सहित 09 पड़ोसी देशों के साथ अपनी सीमा साझा करता है?
questions.explanation: India shares borders with nine countries named Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, China, Maldives, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka. Land Borders: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, China, Myanmar, Nepal, and Pakistan. Sea Borders: Maldives and Sri Lanka. India shares longest border with Bangladesh [4,096.7 km] and shortest with Afghanistan at approximately 106 kilometers. > भारत अफगानिस्तान, बांग्लादेश, भूटान, चीन, मालदीव, म्यांमार, नेपाल, पाकिस्तान और श्रीलंका नाम के नौ देशों के साथ सीमा साझा करता है। भूमि सीमाएँ: अफगानिस्तान, बांग्लादेश, भूटान, चीन, म्यांमार, नेपाल और पाकिस्तान। समुद्री सीमाएँ: मालदीव और श्रीलंका। भारत बांग्लादेश के साथ सबसे लंबी सीमा [4,096.7 किमी] और अफगानिस्तान के साथ सबसे छोटी सीमा लगभग 106 किलोमीटर साझा करता है।

questions.question: Patiala Gharana has which of the following kind/kinds of singing? > पटियाला घराने में निम्नलिखित में से किस प्रकार का गायन है?
options: A and B both>A और B दोनों
None of the above>उपरोक्त में से कोई नहीं
questions.explanation: The Patiala Gharana, a prominent school of Hindustani classical music, is best known for its distinctive style of khayal and thumri singing. It is also notable for its contributions to tappa form. This Gharana places a strong emphasis on intricate ornamentation, elaborate taans [rapid melodic sequences], and soulful expression. It was founded by Mian Kallu, but it gained prominence under the legendary vocalists Ustad Ali Baksh Khan and Ustad Fateh Ali Khan. The Patiala Gharana has produced iconic artists like Ustad Bade Ghulam Ali Khan and his lineage, leaving a lasting mark on Indian classical music. >पटियाला घराना हिंदुस्तानी शास्त्रीय संगीत का एक प्रमुख स्कूल है, जो खयाल और ठुमरी गायन की अपनी विशिष्ट शैली के लिए जाना जाता है। यह टप्पा रूप में अपने योगदान के लिए भी उल्लेखनीय है। यह घराना जटिल अलंकरण, विस्तृत तानों [तेजी से मधुर अनुक्रमों] और भावपूर्ण अभिव्यक्ति पर जोर देता है। इसकी स्थापना मियां कल्लू ने की थी, लेकिन इसे महान गायक उस्ताद अली बख्श खान और उस्ताद फतेह अली खान के तहत प्रमुखता मिली। पटियाला घराने ने उस्ताद बड़े गुलाम अली खान और उनके वंश जैसे प्रतिष्ठित कलाकारों का उत्पादन किया है, जिसने भारतीय शास्त्रीय संगीत पर एक स्थायी छाप छोड़ी है।

questions.question: Which of the following committee is not related to Panchayati Raj system in India? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी समिति भारत में पंचायती राज व्यवस्था से संबंधित नहीं है?

Committees related to Panchayati Raj in India include the Balwant Rai Mehta Committee [1957], Ashok Mehta Committee [1977], G.V.K. questions.question: A hydrocarbon molecule is considered to be saturated if the molecule contains: > एक हाइड्रोकार्बन अणु को संतृप्त माना जाता है यदि अणु में शामिल हैं:
options: A double covalent bond>एक डबल सहसंयोजक बंधन
A triple covalent bond >एक ट्रिपल सहसंयोजक बंधन
Single and double covalent bonds>एकल और डबल सहसंयोजक बंधन
questions.explanation: A hydrocarbon molecule is considered to be saturated if the molecule contains single covalent bonds only. Saturated hydrocarbons, also known as alkanes, consist of carbon and hydrogen atoms connected exclusively by single bonds. This allows the maximum number of hydrogen atoms to be attached to the carbon chain. On the other hand: Molecules with double covalent bonds are called alkenes [unsaturated hydrocarbons] and Molecules with triple covalent bonds are called alkynes [also unsaturated]. >एक हाइड्रोकार्बन अणु को संतृप्त माना जाता है यदि अणु में केवल एकल सहसंयोजक बंधन होते हैं। संतृप्त हाइड्रोकार्बन, जिसे एल्केन्स के रूप में भी जाना जाता है, में कार्बन और हाइड्रोजन परमाणु होते हैं जो विशेष रूप से एकल बंधों से जुड़े होते हैं। यह हाइड्रोजन परमाणुओं की अधिकतम संख्या को कार्बन श्रृंखला से जोड़ने की अनुमति देता है। दूसरी ओर: दोहरे सहसंयोजक बंधों वाले अणुओं को एल्कीन्स [असंतृप्त हाइड्रोकार्बन] कहा जाता है और ट्रिपल सहसंयोजक बंधों वाले अणुओं को अल्काइन्स [असंतृप्त भी] कहा जाता है।

questions.question: Between which two layers of the Earth's crust does the Conrad discontinuity lie?
questions.explanation: The structure of the earth is divided into four major components: the crust, the mantle, the outer core, and the inner core. Each layer has a unique chemical composition, physical state, and can impact life on Earth’s surface. All these layers are separated from each other through a transition zone. These transition zones are called discontinuities. There are five discontinuities/ Transition Zones inside the earth: Conrad Discontinuity: Transition zone between upper and lower Crust. Mohorovicic discontinuity: Transition zone between the Crust and Mantle. Repiti Discontinuity: Transition zone between Outer mantle and Inner mantle. Gutenberg Discontinuity: Transition zone between Mantle and Core. Lehman Discontinuity: Transition zone between Outer core and Inner core.

questions.question: Prasar Bharati launched its OTT platform offering users access to live channels and a vast archive of content from Doordarshan and Akashvani. What is the name of this platform?
questions.explanation: Prasar Bharati launched its OTT platform, named “WAVES”. It offers access to live channels, a vast archive of content from Doordarshan and Aakashvani, movies, TV shows, games, and live events. The platform was launched at the opening ceremony of the 55th International Film Festival of India [IFFI] in Goa. The platform is available in 12+ languages. The platform is free to download and use, with most content available at no cost.

questions.question: Match the following: Padma Vibhushan recipients 2025 Related Field A) Shri Duvvur N Reddy 1) Trade and Industry B) Shri Jagdish Singh Khehar 2) Art C) Smt. Sharda Sinha 3) Medicine D) Shri Osamu Suzuki 4) Public Affairs
questions.explanation: In 2025, the Padma Vibhushan, India’s second-highest civilian award, was conferred upon seven individuals: Justice [Retd.] Jagdish Singh Khehar for Public Affairs, Dr. Duvvur Nageshwar Reddy for Medicine, Kumudini Rajnikant Lakhia for Art, Lakshminarayana Subramaniam for Art, M.T. Vasudevan Nair [posthumously] for Literature and Education, Osamu Suzuki [posthumously] for Trade and Industry, and Sharda Sinha [posthumously] for Art. For the year 2025, the President awarded a total of 139 Padma Awards, which included one duo case [where a joint award counts as one]). The breakdown of the awards was as follows: 7 Padma Vibhushan, 19 Padma Bhushan, and 113 Padma Shri Awards. 23 of the awardees are women and the list also includes 10 persons from the category of Foreigners/NRI/PIO/OCI and 13 posthumous awardees.

questions.question: Where is the tomb of Alauddin Khilji located? > अलाउद्दीन खिलजी का मकबरा कहाँ स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The tomb of Alauddin Khilji is located within the Qutub Minar complex in Mehrauli, Delhi. It is part of an L-shaped madrasa [Islamic school] that he built in 1315. This structure is notable as the first example in India of a tomb combined with a madrasa. > अलाउद्दीन खिलजी का मकबरा दिल्ली के महरौली में कुतुब मीनार परिसर के भीतर स्थित है। यह एक एल-आकार के मदरसे [इस्लामिक स्कूल] का हिस्सा है जिसे उन्होंने 1315 में बनाया था। यह संरचना भारत में मदरसे के साथ संयुक्त मकबरे के पहले उदाहरण के रूप में उल्लेखनीय है।

questions.question: Name the only Indian PM who never faced the Parliament during his tenure? > एकमात्र भारतीय पीएम का नाम बताइए जिन्होंने अपने कार्यकाल के दौरान कभी संसद का सामना नहीं किया?
questions.explanation: Chaudhary Charan Singh was an Indian politician and a freedom fighter who served as the 5th Prime Minister of India from 28 July 1979 to 20 August 1979. He resigned after just 23 days in office [before the vote of no confidence] since Indira Gandhi’s Congress party withdrew support to the government. > चौधरी चरण सिंह एक भारतीय राजनीतिज्ञ और स्वतंत्रता सेनानी थे, जिन्होंने 28 जुलाई 1979 से 20 अगस्त 1979 तक भारत के 5 वें प्रधान मंत्री के रूप में कार्य किया। इंदिरा गांधी की कांग्रेस पार्टी द्वारा सरकार से समर्थन वापस लेने के बाद से उन्होंने कार्यालय में सिर्फ 23 दिनों के बाद [अविश्वास प्रस्ताव से पहले] इस्तीफा दे दिया।

questions.question: Who among the following was also called as “Indian Machiavelli”? > निम्नलिखित में से किसे "भारतीय मैकियावेली" भी कहा जाता था?
options: Raja Ram Mohan Ray>राजा राम मोहन राय
questions.explanation: The title "Indian Machiavelli" is often attributed to Chanakya, also known as Kautilya or Vishnugupta. He was a brilliant strategist, philosopher, and the chief advisor to Chandragupta Maurya, the founder of the Mauryan Empire. Chanakya is renowned for his treatise, the Arthashastra, which is a comprehensive guide on statecraft, economics, and military strategy. >"इंडियन मैकियावेली" शीर्षक का श्रेय अक्सर चाणक्य को दिया जाता है, जिन्हें कौटिल्य या विष्णुगुप्त के नाम से भी जाना जाता है। वह एक शानदार रणनीतिकार, दार्शनिक और मौर्य साम्राज्य के संस्थापक चंद्रगुप्त मौर्य के मुख्य सलाहकार थे। चाणक्य अपने ग्रंथ, अर्थशास्त्र के लिए प्रसिद्ध हैं, जो शासन कला, अर्थशास्त्र और सैन्य रणनीति पर एक व्यापक मार्गदर्शिका है।

questions.question: The Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve is located in? >शीत रेगिस्तान बायोस्फीयर रिजर्व कहाँ स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve is located in the western Himalayas of Himachal Pradesh, India. It was established in August 2009. The reserve is known for its snow leopard conservation efforts. The reserve includes Pin Valley National Park, Chandratal, Sarchu, and Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary. The reserve is a cold desert biome because it's located at a high altitude and is sheltered from monsoon winds. Biosphere reserves are recognized by UNESCO under its Man and the Biosphere [MAB] Programme. >शीत रेगिस्तान बायोस्फीयर रिजर्व भारत के हिमाचल प्रदेश के पश्चिमी हिमालय में स्थित है। इसकी स्थापना अगस्त 2009 में हुई थी। रिजर्व अपने हिम तेंदुए के संरक्षण के प्रयासों के लिए जाना जाता है। रिजर्व में पिन वैली नेशनल पार्क, चंद्रताल, सरचू और किब्बर वन्यजीव अभयारण्य शामिल हैं। रिजर्व एक ठंडा रेगिस्तानी बायोम है क्योंकि यह एक उच्च ऊंचाई पर स्थित है और मानसून हवाओं से आश्रय है। बायोस्फीयर रिजर्व को यूनेस्को द्वारा अपने मैन एंड द बायोस्फीयर [MAB] कार्यक्रम के तहत मान्यता प्राप्त है।

questions.question: The Bhavani Sagar Dam, also known as the Lower Bhavani Dam, is located in which of the following state? > भवानी सागर बांध, जिसे लोअर भवानी बांध के रूप में भी जाना जाता है, निम्नलिखित में से किस राज्य में स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The Bhavani Sagar Dam, also known as the Lower Bhavani Dam, is located in the Erode district of Tamil Nadu, India. It is constructed on the Bhavani River, a significant tributary of the Kaveri River. This dam is one of the world's largest earthen dams and was completed in 1955. It plays a crucial role in irrigation, providing water to the Lower Bhavani Project Canal, and also generates hydroelectric power. The dam is surrounded by picturesque landscapes and even features a garden with recreational facilities, making it a popular spot for visitors. > भवानी सागर बांध, जिसे लोअर भवानी बांध के नाम से भी जाना जाता है, भारत के तमिलनाडु के इरोड जिले में स्थित है। इसका निर्माण कावेरी नदी की एक महत्वपूर्ण सहायक नदी भवानी नदी पर किया गया है। यह बांध दुनिया के सबसे बड़े मिट्टी के बांधों में से एक है और 1955 में बनकर तैयार हुआ था। यह सिंचाई में महत्वपूर्ण भूमिका निभाता है, लोअर भवानी परियोजना नहर को पानी प्रदान करता है, और पनबिजली भी उत्पन्न करता है। बांध सुरम्य परिदृश्यों से घिरा हुआ है और यहां तक कि मनोरंजक सुविधाओं के साथ एक बगीचा भी है, जो इसे आगंतुकों के लिए एक लोकप्रिय स्थान बनाता है।

questions.question: In South India, the most notable mountain pass is the Palakkad Gap, located in which of the following state?
questions.explanation: In South India, the most notable mountain pass is the Palakkad Gap [also known as Palghat Gap], a natural pass in the Western Ghats connecting Kerala and Tamil Nadu. Other Passes in Peninsular India: Amboli Ghat Pass: Connects Sawantwadi in Maharashtra to Belgaum in Karnataka. Amba Ghat Pass: Located in the Sahyadri mountain ranges on the Ratnagiri-Kolhapur route in Maharashtra. Haldighati: Historical mountains pass in the Aravalli Range of Rajasthan. Thal Ghat: Thal Ghat, also known as Kasara Ghat, is a mountain pass in the Western Ghats near Kasara in Maharashtra, India. It is a vital route connecting Mumbai and Nashik, serving both road and rail transportation. Bhor Ghat: Bhor Ghat, also known as the Khandala Ghat, is a historic mountain pass in the Western Ghats of Maharashtra, India. It connects the cities of Mumbai and Pune and serves as a crucial route for both road and rail transportation.

questions.question: Which of the following animals is NOT an amphibian? > निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा जानवर उभयचर नहीं है?
questions.explanation: The animal that is NOT an amphibian among the options is Python. Frogs, Caecilians, and Salamanders are amphibians, meaning they can live both on land and in water. Pythons, on the other hand, are reptiles. They are non-venomous snakes that live exclusively on land and lack the dual habitat adaptation that characterizes amphibians. >विकल्पों में से जो जानवर उभयचर नहीं है, वह अजगर है। मेंढक, कैसिलियन और सैलामैंडर उभयचर हैं, जिसका अर्थ है कि वे जमीन और पानी दोनों में रह सकते हैं। दूसरी ओर, अजगर सरीसृप हैं। ये गैर-विषैले सांप हैं जो विशेष रूप से भूमि पर रहते हैं और दोहरे निवास स्थान अनुकूलन की कमी है जो उभयचरों की विशेषता है।

questions.question: The base financial year for the calculation of the all India Index of Industrial Production [IIP] is: > अखिल भारतीय औद्योगिक उत्पादन सूचकांक [IIP] की गणना के लिए आधार वित्तीय वर्ष है:
questions.explanation: The base financial year for the calculation of the All India Index of Industrial Production [IIP] is 2011-2012. This base year is used to measure and compare industrial growth and performance over time. The Index of Industrial Production [IIP] is an essential indicator for assessing the health of a nation's industrial sector. >अखिल भारतीय औद्योगिक उत्पादन सूचकांक [आईआईपी] की गणना के लिए आधार वित्तीय वर्ष 2011-2012 है। इस आधार वर्ष का उपयोग समय के साथ औद्योगिक विकास और प्रदर्शन को मापने और तुलना करने के लिए किया जाता है। औद्योगिक उत्पादन सूचकांक [आईआईपी] किसी देश के औद्योगिक क्षेत्र के स्वास्थ्य का आकलन करने के लिए एक आवश्यक संकेतक है।

questions.question: Which of the following acid is found in Nettle plant?
questions.explanation: The acid found in nettle plants is methanoic acid [also known as formic acid]. This is responsible for the stinging sensation that occurs when you touch the plant. This acid is stored in hair-like structures on the surface of the nettle, which break off on contact, releasing the acid and causing irritation. >नेटल पौधों में पाया जाने वाला एसिड मेथानोइक एसिड [जिसे फॉर्मिक एसिड भी कहा जाता है] है। यह चुभने वाली सनसनी के लिए जिम्मेदार है जो तब होता है जब आप पौधे को छूते हैं। यह एसिड नेटल की सतह पर बालों जैसी संरचनाओं में जमा होता है, जो संपर्क मे आने पर टूट जाता है, और एसिड जारी करता है और जलन पैदा करता है।

questions.question: Which of the following Indian cities is famous for Kalamkari painting?
questions.explanation: The Indian cities of Srikalahasti and Machilipatnam [Masulipatnam] are famous for Kalamkari painting, with Srikalahasti known for hand-painted Kalamkari and Machilipatnam for block-printed Kalamkari. Kalamkari is traditionally done on cotton fabrics using natural vegetable dyes. > श्रीकालाहस्ती और मछलीपट्टनम [मसुलीपट्टनम] के भारतीय शहर कलमकारी चित्रकला के लिए प्रसिद्ध हैं, श्रीकालाहस्ती को हाथ से पेंट किए गए कलमकारी और मछलीपट्टनम को ब्लॉक-मुद्रित कलमकारी के लिए जाना जाता है। कलमकारी पारंपरिक रूप से प्राकृतिक वनस्पति रंगों का उपयोग करके सूती कपड़ों पर किया जाता है।

questions.question: A 12 V battery is connected across an unknown resistor. If a current of 2 mA is flowing through the circuit, the value of the resistance is _____ .
questions.explanation: To calculate the resistance, you can use Ohm's Law, which states: R=V/I Where: R is the resistance [in ohms], V is the voltage [in volts], I is the current [in amperes]. Given: V =12 V I =2 mA = 0.002 A R=12/0.002=6000 ohms. So, the correct answer is: 6000 ohms.

questions.question: During which five year plan did India opt for a mixed economy? > किस पंचवर्षीय योजना के दौरान भारत ने मिश्रित अर्थव्यवस्था का विकल्प चुना?
options: Fourth Five Year Plan>चौथी पंचवर्षीय योजना
Second Five Year Plan>दूसरी पंचवर्षीय योजना
Third Five Year Plan >तीसरी पंचवर्षीय योजना
First Five Year Plan>पहली पंचवर्षीय योजना
questions.explanation: India opted for a mixed economy during the Second Five Year Plan [1956–1961]. This plan emphasized rapid industrialization, particularly focusing on heavy and basic industries, while balancing the roles of both the public and private sectors. It was based on the Mahalanobis model, which aimed to strengthen the industrial base of the country. >भारत ने दूसरी पंचवर्षीय योजना [1956-1961] के दौरान मिश्रित अर्थव्यवस्था का विकल्प चुना। इस योजना ने सार्वजनिक और निजी दोनों क्षेत्रों की भूमिकाओं को संतुलित करते हुए, विशेष रूप से भारी और बुनियादी उद्योगों पर ध्यान केंद्रित करते हुए तेजी से औद्योगिकीकरण पर जोर दिया। यह महालनोबिस मॉडल पर आधारित था, जिसका उद्देश्य देश के औद्योगिक आधार को मजबूत करना था।

questions.question: Who among the following was finally appointed as the President of the Indian national congress in the year 1939?
questions.explanation: In 1939, Subhash Chandra Bose was elected as the President of the Indian National Congress by defeating Pattabhi Sitaramayya, during the Tripuri Session. However, his presidency faced significant opposition from within the party, particularly from Mahatma Gandhi's faction. Despite winning the election, Bose eventually resigned due to ideological differences and lack of support from the Congress Working Committee and finally Rajendra Prasad appointed as the President of the INC in 1939. > 1939 में, सुभाष चंद्र बोस को त्रिपुरी सत्र के दौरान पट्टाभि सीतारमैया को हराकर भारतीय राष्ट्रीय कांग्रेस के अध्यक्ष के रूप में चुना गया था। हालांकि, उनके अध्यक्ष पद को पार्टी के भीतर से महत्वपूर्ण विरोध का सामना करना पड़ा, खासकर महात्मा गांधी के गुट से। चुनाव जीतने के बावजूद, बोस ने अंततः वैचारिक मतभेदों और कांग्रेस कार्य समिति से समर्थन की कमी के कारण इस्तीफा दे दिया और आखिरकार राजेंद्र प्रसाद को 1939 में कांग्रेस के अध्यक्ष के रूप में नियुक्त किया गया।

questions.question: Which of the given states has highest literacy rate?
questions.explanation: According to given options Mizoram is the highest literate state but as per Census 2011 the highest literate state of India is Kerala with the literacy rate of 94%. Kerala, Lakshadweep, Mizoram, Goa, and Tripura are the top five literate states in India as per the 2011 census. The literacy rate in the country is 74.04 percent, 82.14 for males and 65.46 for females whereas Bihar has lowest literacy rate that was 61.80%. > दिए गए विकल्पों के अनुसार मिजोरम उच्चतम साक्षर राज्य है लेकिन जनगणना 2011 के अनुसार भारत का उच्चतम साक्षर राज्य केरल है जिसकी साक्षरता दर 94% है। 2011 की जनगणना के अनुसार केरल, लक्षद्वीप, मिजोरम, गोवा और त्रिपुरा भारत के शीर्ष पांच साक्षर राज्य हैं। देश में साक्षरता दर 74.04 प्रतिशत, पुरुषों के लिए 82.14 और महिलाओं के लिए 65.46 है जबकि बिहार में सबसे कम साक्षरता दर 61.80% थी।

questions.question: The WADA approved the National Dope Testing Laboratory to manage the ABP for advancement in anti-doping efforts. What is the full form of ABP? > WADA ने डोपिंग रोधी प्रयासों में प्रगति के लिए एबीपी का प्रबंधन करने के लिए राष्ट्रीय डोप परीक्षण प्रयोगशाला को मंजूरी दी। ABP का पूर्ण रूप क्या है?
questions.explanation: The Athlete Biological Passport [ABP] is an advanced anti-doping tool that monitors an athlete’s biological markers over time. By analyzing variations in parameters such as blood and steroid profiles, the ABP helps to ensure fair play in sports and to protect clean athletes. > एथलीट बायोलॉजिकल पासपोर्ट [एबीपी] एक उन्नत एंटी-डोपिंग उपकरण है जो समय के साथ एथलीट के जैविक मार्करों की निगरानी करता है। रक्त और स्टेरॉयड प्रोफाइल जैसे मापदंडों में भिन्नता का विश्लेषण करके, एबीपी खेल में निष्पक्ष खेल सुनिश्चित करने और स्वच्छ एथलीटों की रक्षा करने में मदद करता है।

questions.question: Your body needs vitamins and minerals because __
options: They help carry out metabolic reactions.>वे चयापचय प्रतिक्रियाओं को पूरा करने में मदद करते हैं।
They insulate the body organs.>वे शरीर के अंगों को इन्सुलेट करते हैं।
They give the body energy.>वे शरीर को ऊर्जा देते हैं।
More than one of the above.>उपरोक्त में से एक से अधिक।
questions.explanation: Vitamins and minerals play essential roles in facilitating various metabolic reactions in the body. They act as cofactors and coenzymes that enable chemical reactions necessary for energy production, growth, and overall maintenance of the body. However, they do not provide energy directly like carbohydrates, proteins, or fats, nor do they serve to insulate body organs. > विटामिन और खनिज शरीर में विभिन्न चयापचय प्रतिक्रियाओं को सुविधाजनक बनाने में आवश्यक भूमिका निभाते हैं। वे कॉफ़ैक्टर्स और कोएंजाइम के रूप में कार्य करते हैं जो ऊर्जा उत्पादन, विकास और शरीर के समग्र रखरखाव के लिए आवश्यक रासायनिक प्रतिक्रियाओं को सक्षम करते हैं। हालांकि, वे कार्बोहाइड्रेट, प्रोटीन या वसा की तरह सीधे ऊर्जा प्रदान नहीं करते हैं, न ही वे शरीर के अंगों को इन्सुलेट करने का काम करते हैं।

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in "Its been a long". CONCEPT: • The word "Its" should be the contraction "It's" [meaning "It has"] in this context. The word "Its" is the possessive form of "it", which doesn't fit the meaning here. • The correct structure should use "It's" [short for "It has"], as we are talking about the duration of time that has passed. Correct Sentence: "It's been a long time since we last saw each other."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The Para-Olympics is a competition for the ____________ .
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Disabled Explanation: The Para-Olympics is a competition for athletes with physical or mental impairments, so the appropriate term to fill in the blank is "disabled." Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Disadvantaged – वंचित-In a worse situation due to lack of resources or opportunities. Example: "The disadvantaged children need more support in education." • Discouraged – निराश- Feeling demotivated or lacking confidence. Example: "She was discouraged by the negative feedback." • Disabled – अक्षम- Lacking a physical or mental ability. Example: "The disabled athletes participated in the Para-Olympics." • Despised – नफरत करना / घृणित- Hated or strongly disliked. Example: "He was despised by many for his actions." Conclusion: The best choice is "Disabled" because the Para-Olympics is specifically designed for athletes with disabilities, and the other options do not fit the context.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. My uncle is enough rich to buy a car.
options: rich enough to buy a car.
richer enough to buy a car.
enough richer to buy a car.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: rich enough to buy a car. Explanation: • The correct structure is "adjective + enough", so the sentence should be "rich enough" instead of "enough rich." • The other options [B and C] are incorrect because they use comparative forms "richer" or "enough richer," which are not needed in this context. Thus, the correct sentence is: "My uncle is rich enough to buy a car."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: She will leave as soon as he will arrive.
She will leave as soon as he arrives.
She will leave when he will arrive.
She will leave when he would arrive.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: She will leave as soon as he arrives. Explanation: • When using certain time conjunctions like when, while, if, until, unless, provided that, as soon as, before, after, etc. in sentences that refer to future actions, the verb following these conjunctions is usually in the present tense, not the future tense. • The phrase "as soon as" is a time conjunction. When used in the future context, it should be followed by the present tense verb, not the future tense. This is because the present tense after such conjunctions expresses a future action that is dependent on another event. Therefore, the correct sentence is: "She will leave as soon as he arrives."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Receipt Explanation: Receipt – रसीद – A written acknowledgment of receiving something, typically money or goods. Example: "She handed me the receipt after I made the purchase." Incorrect Spellings and Their Corrections: Primery [Incorrect] – Primary [Correct] – प्रमुख – Relating to the first or most important thing. Example: "The primary goal of the project is to reduce waste." Receed [Incorrect] – Recede [Correct] – पीछे हटना – To move back or away from a previous position. Example: "The flood waters began to recede after the rain stopped." Querrel [Incorrect] – Quarrel [Correct] – झगड़ा – An argument or disagreement. Example: "They had a quarrel over the decision to cancel the event." Conclusion: The best choice is "Receipt" because it is correctly spelled.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. She wanted to speak her mind, but she decided to hold out her thoughts.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Hold back Explanation: • The phrase "hold back" means to restrain or keep something from being expressed or released, which fits perfectly in this context because the person is deciding not to speak her mind. Meanings and Examples of All Options: • Hold back – रोकना / अपने विचारों को दबाना – To restrain or keep oneself from expressing something. Example: "She tried to hold back her tears." • Hold on – पकड़ना / इंतजार करना – To grasp something or wait for a moment. Example: "Please hold on while I check your details." • Hold off – टालना / देरी करना – To delay or postpone something. Example: "We decided to hold off the meeting until next week." • Hold out – प्रस्तुत करना / आशा रखना – To extend something or offer, or to maintain hope. Example: "She tried to hold out her hand in greeting." Corrected Sentence: She wanted to speak her mind, but she decided to hold back her thoughts.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - Everyone looked to see what was causing the COMMOTION.
questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Answer: Disturbance Explanation: The word "commotion" refers to a large, noisy disturbance or uproar, which makes "disturbance" the most appropriate synonym in this context. The sentence suggests that people were trying to understand what caused the noise or disruption. Let's see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Commotion – हलचल – A noisy, chaotic disturbance or uproar. Example: "The commotion in the street drew everyone's attention." • Cheer – उत्साह – A joyful shout or applause, which is the opposite of commotion. Example: "The crowd gave a loud cheer after the victory." • Disturbance – विघटन – A disruption or interference, often noisy or unsettling. Example: "The disturbance in the park alarmed the neighbors." • Imbalance – असंतुलन – A lack of proportion or stability, which doesn't relate to commotion. Example: "The imbalance in the economy led to a financial crisis." • Movement – गति – Refers to motion or change in position, not necessarily a noisy disturbance. Example: "There was a lot of movement at the concert." Conclusion: The correct synonym for "Commotion" is "Disturbance" as both refer to a noisy disruption or interference.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. IMMUNE
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Vulnerable Explanation: The word "immune" refers to being resistant or protected from something, often used in the context of diseases or influences. The opposite of immune would be "vulnerable", which means being susceptible or open to harm or attack. Let's see the meaning of all the options along with their usage: • Immune – प्रतिरक्षित – Resistant or protected from something. Example: "After getting vaccinated, she became immune to the disease." • Vulnerable – संवेदनशील / असुरक्षित – Exposed to the possibility of harm or attack. Example: "Without proper defenses, the country remains vulnerable to attacks." • Free – स्वतंत्र – Not constrained or limited, which is not directly opposite of immune. Example: "She felt free after graduating from college." • Powerful – शक्तिशाली – Having great strength or influence, unrelated to immunity or vulnerability. Example: "The powerful king ruled the land with authority." • Secure – सुरक्षित – Safe from harm, which is a form of being protected, but not exactly the opposite of immune in terms of susceptibility. Example: "The house is secure with strong locks and an alarm system." Conclusion: The correct antonym for "immune" is "vulnerable", as being immune means being resistant to harm, while being vulnerable means being susceptible to harm.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. He shows great ability ______ Mathematics.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: In Explanation: The correct preposition to use with "ability" when referring to a specific subject or field is "in." We use "in" to show someone's proficiency or skill within a particular area, like a subject or activity. Corrected Sentence: "He shows great ability in Mathematics." • Difference Between "At" and "In" When Referring to Being Good in Subjects : When discussing proficiency or skill in a subject, the prepositions "at" and "in" can sometimes be used interchangeably, but there are subtle differences in their usage: "Good at" - Often used with skills or activities that imply a sense of action, performance, or practical application. - Commonly used with verbs like "good at," "skilled at," "talented at." Examples: - "She is good at playing the piano." - "He is excellent at sports." "Good in" - Often used with subjects or fields of study, especially in academic contexts. - Commonly used with subjects like mathematics, science, languages, etc. Examples: - "She is good in mathematics." - "He excels in chemistry." Regional Variations Usage can vary slightly between different English-speaking regions. For instance, "good at" might be more commonly used in American English for both skills and subjects, while "good in" might be preferred in British English for academic subjects. Conclusion: While both "at" and "in" can be used to describe proficiency, "at" tends to emphasize skills or activities, and "in" is often used for academic subjects or fields of study. The choice might also depend on regional language preferences or specific idiomatic expressions.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Get out of hand
options: To give someone a helping hand
To take control of a situation
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: To become uncontrollable Explanation: The idiom "Get out of hand" means that a situation has become uncontrollable or chaotic. It describes circumstances where things have escalated beyond the point of being manageable. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase "get out of hand" likely originates from the idea of losing control over something that you were once holding or managing. If something gets out of your hand, it’s no longer under your control. Example: "The situation at the party got out of hand when the guests started arguing and shouting." It refers to situations that escalate beyond the point of control or become chaotic.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: Error in segment: There has never been CONCEPT: • The error lies in subject-verb agreement. The phrase "There has never been" is singular, and it should be followed by a singular noun. However, "so many cases" is plural, which causes a mismatch in subject-verb agreement. • The correct structure should be "There have never been" to agree with the plural noun "cases." Corrected Sentence: "There have never been so many cases of polio in a single district."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. ADVANCE
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Progress. Explanation: The word "advance" means to move forward or make progress in a particular field or toward a goal. The synonym that most closely matches this meaning is "progress", which refers to forward movement or development. Let’s see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Advance – प्रगति करना / आगे बढ़ना – To move forward or make progress. Example: "The company is making great advances in technology." • Reduce – घटाना / कम करना – To make something smaller or less in amount, degree, or size. Example: "We need to reduce the amount of waste we produce." • Halt – रुकना / ठहरना – To stop or come to an end. Example: "The train had to halt due to a technical issue." • Progress – प्रगति करना / आगे बढ़ना – Forward movement toward a goal or improvement. Example: "The project is progressing smoothly and on schedule." • Extend – बढ़ाना / विस्तारित करना – To make something longer in time or space. Example: "They decided to extend the deadline for the project." Conclusion: The correct synonym for "advance" is "progress", as both words convey the idea of moving forward or making improvement.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- He made a FEEBLE attempt at a smile.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Robust. Explanation: The word "feeble" means weak or lacking strength. The most appropriate antonym for "feeble" would be a word that means strong or powerful, and "robust" fits perfectly. Robust refers to something that is strong, healthy, or vigorous. Let’s see the meaning of all the words along with their usage: • Feeble – कमजोर – Lacking strength or vitality. Example: "His attempt to lift the heavy box was feeble and unsuccessful." • Rickety – झूलते हुए / कमजोर – In poor condition and likely to collapse or break. Example: "The old car was rickety, and I was worried it might break down any moment." • Weak – कमजोर – Lacking strength, power, or resilience. Example: "He felt weak after the long illness." • Infirm – दुर्बल – Lacking physical strength, especially due to age or illness. Example: "The infirm elderly man needed help crossing the street." • Robust – मजबूत / शक्तिशाली – Strong and healthy; able to withstand stress or difficult conditions. Example: "She is robust and can handle even the most challenging situations." Conclusion: The correct antonym for "feeble" is "robust", as it refers to something strong or powerful, directly opposing the idea of weakness implied by "feeble."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. He has been pruning the _________ trees.
questions.explanation: The sentence should read: "He has been pruning the pear trees." Explanation of the Other Options: • Pair – जोड़ / जोड़ा – Refers to two things that are used together. Example: "I bought a pair of shoes." • Pear – नाशपाती – A type of fruit. Example: "She ate a pear for breakfast." • Pare – छांटना / काटना – To trim or remove the outer layer. Example: "She had to pare the apples before making the pie." • Pyre – चिता – A large structure for burning a dead body, especially in funerals. Example: "The body was placed on the pyre and set on fire." Conclusion: The correct word is "Pear", as it refers to the type of fruit, and the sentence talks about pruning trees, which are typically fruit trees like pear trees.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Having a common centre
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Concentric. Here are the explanations for all options: • Concentric – समानकेंद्रीय – Having a common centre or axis, especially when referring to circles or spheres that share a common centre. Example: "The target had concentric circles, with each one inside the other." • Chronic – दीर्घकालिक / लगातार – Refers to a condition or situation that persists for a long time, especially in the context of diseases or problems. Example: "He suffers from chronic back pain." • Circular – गोलाकार – Refers to something having the shape of a circle, but it doesn't necessarily imply having a common centre like concentric does. Example: "The table is circular in shape." • Eccentric – विचित्र / असामान्य – Refers to something that is unconventional or unusual in behaviour or design, often not cantered or symmetrical. Example: "His eccentric behaviour made him stand out at the party." Conclusion: The correct word is Concentric, as it specifically refers to shapes [like circles or spheres] that share a common centre.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. He had to pay through his nose to get the job done.
options: Pay an excessive amount of money
Undergo the treatment of this nose
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pay an excessive amount of money. Explanation: The idiom "pay through one's nose" means to pay a lot of money for something, usually more than it is worth, or to pay an excessive amount. This phrase is used when someone has to pay an unusually high price for a service or product, often due to circumstances that force them to do so. Origin of the Idiom: The origin of the idiom "pay through one's nose" is unclear, but one possible explanation is that it refers to an old punishment where people were forced to pay heavy fines or taxes. Over time, it evolved to mean paying excessively for something. Example: "He had to pay through his nose to get the job done because of the urgent deadline." Conclusion: The meaning of "pay through one's nose" is best described as "pay an excessive amount of money," indicating that someone has to pay a lot, often more than expected or reasonable.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A person who readily believes others
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Credulous. The sentence should read: "A person who readily believes others is called Credulous." Explanation of the Other Options: • Sensible – समझदार / विवेकपूर्ण – Someone who uses good judgment, not easily deceived. Example: "She made a sensible choice to avoid the risky investment." • Sensitive – संवेदनशील – Refers to someone easily affected by emotions, not necessarily easily deceived. Example: "He is sensitive to criticism." • Credible – विश्वसनीय – Something or someone trustworthy, but not a person who easily believes others. Example: "The report was credible and based on solid facts." • Credulous – विश्वास करने वाला – Someone who believes things too easily, without questioning. Example: "The credulous man trusted every word he heard." Conclusion: The correct word is "Credulous," as it describes someone who is easily deceived or believes others readily.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. The sun is very important for life on Earth. B. It provides light and heat to plants and animals. C. Without the sun, Earth would be very cold and dark. D. Plants use sunlight to make food through photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: ABDC This order makes sense because: • Sentence A introduces the topic: The sun is very important for life on Earth. • Sentence B explains its significance: It provides light and heat to plants and animals. • Sentence D elaborates further on how plants benefit from the sun: Plants use sunlight to make food through photosynthesis. • Sentence C provides a consequence of not having the sun: Without the sun, Earth would be very cold and dark. Final paragraph: The sun is very important for life on Earth. It provides light and heat to plants and animals. Plants use sunlight to make food through photosynthesis. Without the sun, Earth would be very cold and dark.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. They clean the park every morning.
options: The park is cleaned every morning.
The park has cleaned every morning.
The park was cleaned every morning.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The given sentence follows the active voice structure: Subject + Verb + Object → They clean the park every morning. • To convert it into passive voice, we use the structure: Object + am/are/is + past participle + by + Subject. • "The park" becomes the object, and the verb "clean" changes to the past participle "cleaned." • The appropriate passive form uses "is" because the action occurs regularly every morning [present tense]. Conclusion: Applying this rule, the passive form becomes: "The park is cleaned every morning."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He said, “She had been weeping for an hour.”
options: He says that she was weeping for an hour.
He said that she was weeping for an hour.
He said that she has been weeping for an hour.
He said that she had been weeping for an hour.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: He said that she had been weeping for an hour. Explanation: • The given sentence is in direct speech and follows the structure: He said, “She had been weeping for an hour.” • To convert it into indirect speech, follow these rules: o The reporting verb "said" stays as "said" because it's a statement. o The present perfect continuous tense ["had been weeping"] remains unchanged as "had been weeping" in the past tense. o No pronoun changes are required in this case as "she" remains the same. Corrected Sentence: "He said that she had been weeping for an hour."
